findings.description: The introduction of a dedicated 'validator' archetype (D2) correctly separates the concerns of 'producing evidence' from 'modifying source code', resolving the contract contradiction while maintaining safety boundaries.

Nit: Substring-based read-only checks (e.g., 'without making changes') in Requirement: VALIDATE-phase Archetype Must Be Write-Capable are brittle and might miss semantic equivalents or fail on harmless uses of the phrase.
findings.resolution: Consider augmenting the check with a more comprehensive list of forbidden phrases or documenting that the check is a heuristic for the specific failure mode observed.

findings.description: Nit: The error message for phase mismatch in apply-outcome (Requirement: apply-outcome rejects phase mismatch) should explicitly mention the existence of the --force flag to guide operators during manual recovery.
findings.resolution: Update the requirement or Task 3.2 to specify that the error message includes a hint about using --force for manual overrides.

findings.description: Optional: The state-mutation prohibition (Layer B) is only applied to write-capable phases. While read-only phases currently map to 'analyst', applying the prohibition to all autopilot-managed sub-agents would provide better defense-in-depth against future archetype mapping changes.
findings.resolution: Apply the prompt prohibition in Task 4.1 to all autopilot sub-agent dispatch prompts, regardless of their current write-capability classification.
